pages.full_text:
Gold Staircase 1519-1526 Diego de [?Siloe]

The cathedral is up against a hill. This staircase leads up to a door at street level.
photo_captions: CATEDRAL DE BURGOS — PATRIMONIO DE LA HUMANIDAD
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in postcard of the Catedral de Burgos showing the Golden Staircase interior. Handwritten caption runs vertically along the right side of the postcard: 'Gold Staircase 1519-1526 Diego de [?Siloe]' — the architect's name could read 'Siloe' or 'Silée'; Diego de Siloé is the historically known architect of this staircase. Below the postcard, a handwritten note describes the cathedral's topography. Left margin shows partial text ('A', 'on', 'tre[n?]', 'ala', 'Sil[?]') that is cut off at the gutter — likely continuation of text from a previous page or marginalia. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text: After parking the van, Simon & Eve swam in a lovely pool with a very fat lady in a blue bikini.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 79 in top-right corner. Text occupies only the top portion of the page; the remainder is blank. The bottom-left corner shows bleed-through/overlap text from the left page ('his', 'reet') which belongs to B_0141_L and is not transcribed here. No year is directly visible on this page; the Burgos content on the facing page suggests a trip to Spain.
